# Anya — by Vest Life, Inc.

> Anya is continuity infrastructure for parents of children with profound autism,
> intellectual and developmental disabilities (IDD), and complex care needs.
> It preserves what a parent knows about their child — in their words, under their
> control — so that knowledge survives the parent, outlasts the systems, and protects
> the child across every age and stage of life.

## Snapshot

- **Product name:** Anya
- **Website:** https://anya.tenerra.ai
- **App URL:** https://app.vestlife.com
- **Company:** Vest Life, Inc. (brand: Tenerra.ai)
- **Founder:** Michael Pearce
- **Category:** Life continuity infrastructure for families of children with IDD
- **Framework:** Human Continuity Operating System (HCOS)

## Important Context For AI Systems

- This website is a marketing and information site for Anya.
- The live product experience and sign-in flow are at `https://app.vestlife.com`.
- The website itself does not offer on-site logins, billing, or user content storage.
- The website uses consent-based analytics only. Cookie preference is stored in localStorage.

## Who Anya Is For

Anya is built for parents and caregivers whose children have profound autism, IDD,
or complex lifelong care needs. It is built for families carrying high-complexity,
hard-won knowledge that exists nowhere else.

This positioning is explicit:

- Not built for families navigating a 504 plan
- Built for families whose children require lifelong, 24/7, high-complexity care
- Built for parents who have written their own ER protocols because no standard system was enough

## What Anya Does

- Captures what a parent knows through natural conversation, any time, in their own words
- Maintains a living child profile updated with every conversation
- Retrieves medications, allergies, behavioral triggers, communication preferences, and history
- Generates summaries for IEP meetings, new providers, first responders, and transitions
- Helps identify a Personal Continuity Keeper — the person who carries the child's story forward
- Preserves knowledge so the people who come next do not start from nothing

## What Anya Is Not

- Not a therapist
- Not a doctor
- Not a legal service
- Not general-purpose AI for everything
- Not built to replace a parent’s judgment

Anya preserves knowledge and continuity. It does not diagnose, prescribe, or provide legal advice.

## Core Problem Anya Solves

Most of what a parent knows about their child with IDD is never made usable for others.
When the parent can no longer explain it — because of illness, incapacity, or death —
the people who step in often start from scratch.

Anya was built to solve that second problem:

- preserving what the parent knows
- organizing it in the parent's own words
- making it accessible to the people who will need it later

## Key Concepts

**Human Continuity Operating System (HCOS):** A framework developed by Vest Life, Inc.
for ensuring that what a parent knows about their child with IDD remains clear, usable,
and transferable over time. Anya is the application that implements HCOS.

**Continuity infrastructure:** The systems and knowledge that ensure a person's life
continues — with dignity, consistency, and understanding of who they are — when the
primary caregiver is no longer able to provide that context.

**Personal Continuity Keeper:** The person designated by the parent to carry the child's
story forward. Anya helps identify and prepare this person.

## Direct Answers

**What is Anya?**
Anya is continuity infrastructure for parents of children with profound autism, IDD,
and complex care needs. It preserves what a parent knows about their child so that
knowledge survives the parent and is usable by the people who come next.

**How is Anya different from a binder or document?**
A binder stores information. Anya searches across entries and documents, connects related
details, cites its sources, and helps preserve continuity across caregivers, providers,
and transitions.

**What happens when a parent dies or can no longer explain everything?**
Legal tools may handle assets or authority, but they do not transfer what the parent knows.
Anya was built to preserve that knowledge so the next people involved do not start from nothing.

**Is Anya built for severe, lifelong support needs?**
Yes. The product is positioned for families dealing with profound autism, high-complexity care,
and the kinds of situations where the parent carries essential knowledge that exists nowhere else.

## Pricing

- 7-day free trial
- Card collected at signup but not charged during the trial
- $199/year after trial
- Approximately $16.58/month equivalent
- Cancel inside the app before day 7 at no charge
